'''Jack Ashworth''' (born 3 July 1995) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level.<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=24 October 2019}}</ref>

He has previously played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time on loan or dual registration at [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[2015 Championship 1|Championship 1]], and at [[Sheffield Eagles]], [[Leigh Centurions]], the [[Halifax Panthers]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

==Background==
Jack Ashworth was born in [[Rochdale, Greater Manchester]], [[England]], On Monday 3rd July 1995. 

==Education==
Jack Ashworth attended [[St Cuthbert's RC Business and Enterprise College]] in [[Rochdale]] from 2006 until 2011. He then went on to do a sports course at Hopwood Hall College Middleton.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

==Career==
===St Helens===
Ashworth made his Saints debut on 6 April 2015 in a [[Super League]] match against [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>[http://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/Jack-Ashworth/ Love Rugby League]</ref>

During 2015, Ashworth also played for [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] on [[RFL dual registration|dual registration]].

In the [[Super League XXI|2016 season]], Ashworth played an outstanding game against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], and made a Man of the Match performance against [[Hull F.C.]]

Ashworth joined up with the [[Sheffield Eagles]] on dual registration on 31 January 2018, ahead of their first Championship match against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]].<ref name="Saints quartet become first dual-registrated players in 2018">{{cite web|url=http://www.sheffieldeagles.com/article/50794/saints-quartet-become-first-dual-registered-players-in-|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180201075339/http://www.sheffieldeagles.com/article/50794/saints-quartet-become-first-dual-registered-players-in-|url-status=dead|archive-date=1 February 2018|title=Ashworth joins Eagles on dual-registration|date=31 January 2018|accessdate=31 January 2018}}</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup final]] defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|accessdate=1 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|accessdate=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019 |last1=Unwin |first1=Will }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 30 November 2020, it was announced that Ashworth had signed a two-year deal with [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55129544|title=Jack Ashworth: Huddersfield Giants sign St Helens forward|work=BBC Sport |accessdate=4 December 2020}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions (loan)===
On 11 July 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the [[Super League]] on season-long loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 Jul 2021|accessdate=11 Jul 2021|title=Leigh confirm player-swap deals with Huddersfield|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-confirm-player-swap-deals-with-huddersfield/}}</ref>

In round 17 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he was sent off for fighting in Leigh's 50–6 loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58049907|title=Wigan Warriors thump winless Leigh Centurions 50-6|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
On 5 October 2023, it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull F.C.]] for the 2024 season on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=5 Oct 2023|accessdate=8 Oct 2023|title=Hull FC land Super League Grand Final winner: Forward 'can't wait' to play under new boss Tony Smith|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-fc-land-super-league-grand-final-winner-forward-cant-wait-to-play-under-new-boss-tony-smith}}</ref>
On 10 September 2025, Ashworth was suspended for seven matches after being found guilty by the match tribunal for striking [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] player [[Mikey Lewis]] in the face during Hull F.C.'s derby loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cm2z38d6y3go/|title=Ashworth and Maria given lengthy Super League bans|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
On 28 September 2025, it was announced that he had signed for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=28 Sep 2025|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=Castleford Tigers sign Jack Ashworth from Hull FC|url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-sign-jack-ashworth-from-hull-fc/}}</ref>

==International career==
In 2019, he was selected for the [[England Knights]] against {{Rlnt|Jamaica}} at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].<ref name="England Knights vs Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights vs Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>
